verificationNote: Requirements constructed from IEC 62443, NIST SP 800-82, and NACSA Act 854 obligations. Not endorsed by any standards body — verify against authoritative sources before use in formal assessments.
name: Monitoring and Logging
description: Comprehensive logging and monitoring requirements for OT environments. Covers audit log generation per OT component, centralised log collection, SIEM integration, and the specific OT monitoring considerations (passive-only, no active probing, OT protocol-aware analysis).

requirements.name: OT log sources and collection
requirements.description: Identify all OT log sources and implement centralised log collection. OT log sources include: Windows event logs from HMI/EWS, SCADA application logs, historian change logs, OT network device logs, firewall logs, and OT monitoring platform alerts.

technical.summary: Centralised syslog collection from all OT network devices and Windows-based OT systems. OT monitoring platform events forwarded to SIEM.
actions: Deploy centralised syslog server in IDMZ (not in OT zone — write-only from OT perspective)
Configure syslog forwarding from: OT firewalls, OT switches, HMI workstations, SCADA servers, historian server, engineering workstations
Forward OT monitoring platform alerts to SIEM
Retain logs minimum 90 days locally; 1 year in central SIEM; 7 years for incident-related logs (NACSA s26)
Define minimum log events per source: authentication events, configuration changes, service starts/stops, security software events

governance.summary: Log collection coverage reviewed quarterly. Gaps documented as risk items.
actions: Quarterly log coverage assessment
Log retention compliance monitored
Log infrastructure availability monitored — loss of logging is a security event

evidenceItems: Log source register with collection status

slMapping.sl2: Centralised syslog; 90-day minimum retention; authentication and change events captured
slMapping.sl3: Centralised SIEM with OT platform integration; real-time correlation; 1-year retention
slMapping.sl4: Immutable log storage; forensic-quality log preservation; 7-year retention for incident logs

requirements.name: SIEM integration and OT correlation rules
requirements.description: Integrate OT log sources with the Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) platform and implement OT-specific correlation rules that detect OT attack patterns.

technical.summary: SIEM includes OT-specific detection rules: unusual engineering connections, off-hours PLC programming access, new device appearing on OT network, authentication failures on SCADA, large data transfers from OT to IT.
actions: Onboard all OT log sources to SIEM with appropriate parsers for OT log formats

Implement OT-specific detection rules: new device on OT network, off-hours engineering access, PLC program read/write outside change window, unusual data volumes from historian to IT side, authentication failures on OT systems, SCADA service restarts
Correlate OT events with IT events (e.g., compromise on IT + unusual OT access = potential lateral movement)
Tune detection rules against OT operational patterns to reduce false positives (OT SIEM needs OT expertise to tune effectively)
Define response playbooks for each OT alert type

governance.summary: SIEM OT correlation rules reviewed quarterly by OT security and SOC teams.
actions: Quarterly rule review and tuning
Monthly detection effectiveness report
Rule updates when new OT threat intelligence received

slMapping.sl2: Basic SIEM with OT log sources; manual review
slMapping.sl3: OT-specific correlation rules; automated alerting; SOC with OT expertise
slMapping.sl4: Dedicated OT threat detection platform; AI/ML behavioural analysis; 24/7 OT SOC

requirements.name: Log retention — tiered retention aligned to NACSA s26
requirements.description: OT log retention must be tiered to meet both operational and legal obligations. NACSA Act 854 s26 imposes a 7-year record-keeping obligation for cybersecurity incident records. Operational logs require shorter retention but must be accessible for correlation and investigation.

legal.summary: NACSA s26(6) requires records of cybersecurity incidents to be retained for 7 years. IEC 62443 SR-2.9 requires audit log retention sufficient to support incident investigation.

technical.summary: Three-tier retention: hot (90 days, SIEM — fast query), warm (1 year, log archive), cold (7 years, immutable, write-once storage for incident-related logs). Immutability protects logs as legal evidence.
actions: Define three retention tiers: 90 days in SIEM (hot), 1 year in log archive (warm), 7 years immutable for incident-classified logs (cold)
Implement write-once/append-only storage for cold tier (WORM storage, S3 Object Lock, or equivalent)
Automate tiering from SIEM to archive at 90 days
Tag all logs related to a declared security incident — retain tagged logs for 7 years from incident close date
Document retention policy, storage locations, and responsible custodian
Annual retention compliance audit — verify logs are present and retrievable for spot-checked dates

governance.summary: Log retention policy reviewed annually. Legal counsel confirms s26 obligations met. Retention compliance audited annually.
actions: Annual log retention compliance audit
Log custodian designated and documented
Retention policy reviewed when regulations change

evidenceItems: Log retention policy (tiered — 90 days / 1 year / 7 years)
Incident log tagging and retention procedure

slMapping.sl3: Tiered retention implemented; incident logs tagged and extended; annual compliance audit
slMapping.sl4: Immutable WORM storage for all retained logs; tamper-evident log chain; forensic-grade preservation

requirements.name: OT threat hunting
requirements.description: Proactive threat hunting in OT environments to detect adversary TTPs that evade automated alerting. OT threat hunting uses MITRE ATT&CK for ICS as the framework for hypothesis-driven searches across OT log data and network traffic.

legal.summary: IEC 62443 SR-6.2 requires continuous security monitoring including capabilities that can detect compromises that automated signatures miss. Threat hunting fulfils the proactive detection intent of SR-6.2.
legal.owner: OT Security Team / SOC (with OT expertise)

technical.summary: Quarterly threat hunt exercises using MITRE ATT&CK for ICS TTP hypotheses. Hunters review OT network baselines, log anomalies, and endpoint artefacts to find activity that automated rules did not alert on.
actions: Define threat hunting programme: quarterly hunts, hypothesis-driven using MITRE ATT&CK for ICS

Priority hunting hypotheses for Malaysian OT environments: T0886 (Remote Services exploitation), T0812 (Default Credentials), T0829 (Loss of Safety — pre-conditions), T0855 (Unauthorised Command Message), T0862 (Supply Chain Compromise indicators)
For each hunt: define hypothesis → identify data sources needed → search for indicators → document findings → update detection rules based on hunt outcomes
Require OT network monitoring platform data (Claroty/Dragos/Nozomi) for effective OT hunting — IT SIEM alone is insufficient
Document null-finding hunts — absence of evidence from a thorough hunt has evidential value
Brief findings to OT manager; update SIEM rules based on hunt outcomes

governance.summary: Threat hunt schedule approved by OT security manager. Findings briefed to OT manager. SIEM rule updates tracked from hunt findings.
actions: Quarterly hunt schedule maintained
Hunt report per exercise (hypothesis, data searched, finding, outcome)
Hunt findings drive SIEM rule updates — tracked via change management

evidenceItems: Hunt reports per exercise (hypothesis, scope, findings, outcome)
MITRE ATT&CK for ICS technique coverage map
SIEM rule updates traceable to hunt findings

slMapping.sl2: Ad-hoc review of OT logs for anomalies; no formal programme
slMapping.sl3: Quarterly threat hunting programme using ATT&CK for ICS; documented hunts; SIEM rule updates
slMapping.sl4: Continuous threat hunting as part of 24/7 OT SOC; dedicated OT threat hunter role; monthly hunt cadence

requirements.name: SOC integration for OT
requirements.description: OT security events must be integrated into Security Operations Centre (SOC) monitoring with OT-aware analyst capability. The SOC must understand OT operational context — what is normal activity and what is a security event — to avoid both false positives that disrupt operations and false negatives that miss real attacks.

legal.summary: SR-6.1 and SR-6.2 require continuous monitoring and audit log accessibility. For OT environments with 24/7 operations, SOC integration provides the personnel capability to act on monitoring outputs at all times.

technical.summary: OT events routed to SOC with OT-specific playbooks per alert type. SOC analysts receive OT security awareness training. Escalation path from SOC to OT security team and OT operations defined and tested.
actions: Route OT SIEM alerts to SOC with OT-specific context in alert metadata (zone, asset type, operational significance)
Define SOC escalation path for OT events: SOC L1 → OT security lead → OT operations manager → NACSA notification coordinator

Develop OT-specific SOC playbooks: 1) Unauthorised device on OT network 2) Off-hours engineering access 3) PLC program modification 4) SCADA authentication failure storm 5) OT system availability loss
OT security awareness training for SOC analysts (minimum: Purdue model, OT protocol basics, OT false positive patterns, who to call for OT context)
Monthly joint IT/OT SOC review meeting: review OT alerts, tune rules, update playbooks
Critical requirement: SOC must NOT take active response actions in OT (no blocking, no device isolation) without OT operations approval — operational safety first

governance.summary: OT-SOC integration governed by OT/SOC interface agreement. Monthly joint review. Annual SOC OT training refresh.
actions: OT/SOC interface agreement defining responsibilities, escalation, and constraints
Monthly joint OT/SOC review meeting with agenda and minutes
Annual OT security awareness training for all SOC analysts
NACSA s26 notification coordinator identified and trained

evidenceItems: SOC alert routing configuration for OT events
OT awareness training records for SOC analysts
Monthly joint OT/SOC review meeting minutes

slMapping.sl2: OT alerts in SIEM; SOC monitors with OT escalation contact; basic playbooks
slMapping.sl3: OT-specific SOC playbooks; trained SOC analysts; monthly joint review; tested escalation path
slMapping.sl4: Dedicated OT SOC function with OT-certified analysts; 24/7 OT monitoring; sub-1-hour NACSA notification readiness
